prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for furan were computed using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 (neutral), with cation at charge +1 / multiplicity 2 and anion at charge −1 / multiplicity 2. Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied furan.xyz (single-point; not re-optimized at DFT). Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme in PySCF. The molecule has 9 atoms (O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H).

The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (electrophilic attack Fukui index) = 1.9242931 at atom 5 (C, 1-based index), identifying the α-carbon (C2/C5 position) as the most electrophilic site. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻ = 0.1616371) also falls at atom 5. The dual descriptor at atom 5 is +1.762656, confirming it as the dominant ambiphilic/electrophilic-attack-favoured site. Both charge-conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity: trustworthy = true. Plot written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260702-201026_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
